Xiaotu Review - Empires and Puzzles Lunar New Year Hero Strategy Guide

Xiaotu may look cute, but this rabbit is a nightmare for summoners and healers alike. Her stats are aging, but her passive ability remains one of the best in the game. Does she still have a place on your team in 2026? Let's hop into the details.
Xiaotu Review: The Rabbit Who Silences Minions
Xiaotu, The Lunar Avenger: Silencing Minions and Reversing Recovery

, hopped into Empires & Puzzles during the 2023 Lunar New Year event. As a Fire (Red) Legendary hero, she introduced a mechanic that fundamentally changed how we deal with summoner-heavy defenses: a passive ability that completely shuts down enemy minions. While she operates at
speed, her ability to punish healing and neutralize minion effects makes her a unique tactical asset, even years after her release. She's not just a sniper; she's a silencer.

Xiaotu's design is a vibrant celebration of the Lunar New Year. Clad in festive red and gold traditional attire, she wields a weapon that looks like a fireworks launcher or a stylized staff. The artwork captures a blend of determination and festivity—she's clearly a warrior, but the bunny ears and fluffy tail add a whimsical touch. The card border, featuring the intricate Lunar New Year motifs, remains one of the more distinct frames in the game. It's a design that says "celebration" but screams "I will destroy your minions."
Xiaotu comes from the era of roughly ~860 Power. Let's look at her base stats compared to modern behemoths.
By 2026 standards, Xiaotu's stats are undeniably outdated.

These defensive stats are now perilously low. Modern tanks and even attackers boast 1300+ Defense and 2200+ Health. Xiaotu is fragile and requires careful protection (Limit Breaking +20 emblems) to survive a single hit from a modern sniper.
"Reverse Recovery" is a tactical nuke with a nasty side effect.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
This section is where Xiaotu makes her case for staying on your roster.
The Zodiac family bonus is unique. Xiaotu gives
. You need two unique heroes to activate this. It's a nice perk if you run a themed team, but rarely a game-changer on its own.
As long as Xiaotu is alive, enemy minions are just meat shields. They cannot attack. They cannot apply status effects (like
healing). This single passive completely neutralizes the threat of many top-tier minion summoners. It is arguably one of the strongest counters in the game.

. Simple, effective. It boosts her base attack at the start of the battle, helping her 655% sniper hit land with more authority. It's not as flashy as "Bulwark" or "Dodge," but it suits her role as a killer.
Upon release, Xiaotu was a direct answer to the "Minion Meta" dominated by heroes like
. She was a must-have for wars involving those tanks.
Statistically, she has fallen off a cliff. New 4-star heroes (like
) have comparable or better survivability. However, her
has immune-to-power-creep properties. Disabling minion effects is binary: it works or it doesn't. And it still works perfectly.

You don't bring Xiaotu for her stats; you bring her to shut down specific mechanics. If the enemy relies on minions for mana control or ailments, Xiaotu is still a VIP. In a vacuum, she is too squishy for top 100 defense, but on offense, she is a scalpel.
Her passive is her best asset. She needs to stay alive to keep minions silenced. Modern snipers will delete her if she stays at 819 Defense.

If you pulled Xiaotu, congrats! She is a "problem solver." Don't use her as your main tank or defense hero. Keep her for when you face a team that spawns lots of annoying little minions. Max her out, but prioritize survivability emblems.
You likely have her benched for newer, shinier toys with 1300 stats. However, don't dust her off just yet. Check your War matchups. If you see a

tank in 2026, Xiaotu is still arguably the cleanest counter. Is she worth Alpha Aethers (LB2)? Probably not, unless you use her daily. Save those for modern stat-monsters. LB1 is sufficient for her niche utility.
When released, the community went wild over her passive.
"I really hate the passive. It’s a lazy, in your face way to say ‘look we solve the waterpipe problem'."
Players immediately recognized her as a hard counter to the "Minion Meta."
Players debated if she was better than
(the Tiger). While Laohu is faster, Xiaotu's healing punishment was often seen as the more devastating ailment for offense.

Xiaotu is a classic example of "Utility over Stats." In a game defined by power creep, her stats are ancient history. But her ability to tell enemy minions to "sit down and shut up" is timeless. She effectively removes an entire mechanic from the enemy team just by showing up. She might be a glass cannon now, but she's a glass cannon that silences the annoying noise of the battlefield.
A permanent niche tool for your offensive war chest. Keep her fed, keep her safe, and let the rabbit hunt.
A comprehensive review of Xiaotu, the Lunar New Year Fire sniper. Discover her unique anti-healing mechanics, minion-silencing passive, and modern viability.
